key_insight: An exact hyperfine-spin relaxation model shows that localized carrier spins in metal-halide perovskites relax through distinct nuclear channels, with holes typically coupling more strongly than electrons in Pb-based materials.
engineering_relevance: The model helps predict and tune spin lifetimes by changing halogen/metal composition and supports extraction of carrier localization and correlation times from spin dynamics measurements.
method: Adapted a muon-spin-relaxation framework to localized carriers in perovskites.
Solved longitudinal spin relaxation exactly across arbitrary hyperfine correlation times.
Compared the model against time-resolved Faraday rotation data from two perovskite samples.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that fitting time-resolved Faraday rotation data from two samples yields localization volume and hyperfine correlation time, and shows the exact solution is needed because a single-exponential approximation fails in the intermediate regime.
watch_item: The framework targets localized carriers and may be less directly applicable if carrier delocalization, additional relaxation pathways, or strong sample heterogeneity dominate.
paper_title: Hyperfine driven spin relaxation of charge carriers in metal-halide perovskites
